title: Whynter BR-130SB Beverage Refrigerator with Internal Fan – Stainless Steel 120-Can Capacity

stopped_working: Was great until it died less than 2 years after we bought it

It broke after 7 months of use and they want $75 to fix it and me to pay shipping to them and $55 to ship it back

The item cost $200 new

Junk! Poor customer service. Very disappointed

Bought it in June - Stopped working in November
Now we will see if the 2 yr Assurant 360 Plan is worth anything ....
cooling_failure: I purchased in January with a three year warranty that has mysteriously disappeared on Amazon it quit working on November 21st just before Thanksgiving.  Less then a year and still trying to find out what protection I had. It will no longer go under 50 degrees. To boot the 3 year protection site wont recognize order number for either the plan or the stupid fridge. No way to get ahold of anybody ,

Received this as a gift a year ago to use as a beer fridge purchased thru Amazon. Product came with dents, but not too many. Given the cost to return I decided to keep it.  At first it cooled if not loaded too heavy at once. After a couple months, it didn't cool as well. After 10 months, it has quit cooling completely.  It runs but doesn't cool. physical_breakage: Loch broke after two weeks, had to spend 30 minutes to get it open after turning it upside down.

I wanted to love this refrigerator so bad. Gave it to my husband as a Christmas gift for his man cave. He notified me a few hours later that the door doesn't close. Sure enough it's been a week and still waiting on the replacement.
noise_vibration: We were happy with our Whynter mini fridge for about 1.5 years.  it was performing well, then all of a sudden the motor has been making an extremely loud noises a few times a day.  It is like an airplane taking off. Because we are schooling and working from home, i will need to replace this refrigerator with another.  Very disappointed.

While I was initially very happy about this purchase, I've encountered two negatives. One small, and one huge.
The small one was simply a design issue, I'm sure they made the decision based on economics. The interior light has to be turned on with a switch, it does not activate when the door opens. No big deal, it's kind of a cheap fridge, I guess that just goes with the territory.
The s

After a year and a half, my Whynter BR-128WS Beverage Refrigerator began making an extremely loud, reverberating sound and overheating. I turned the fridge off and this continued. I unplugged the device and it finally stopped. This happened a day before I left for vacation. Had it happened one day later, my house likely would have burned down.

Things happen in life and even the best
motor_failure: Worked good until today, 5 months after I bought it and the motor started making a loud noise and before I could unplug it it just died. Purchased on 1/29/21 died on 6/11/21...
Two years and the fan died. Can’t get a response for parts.
leak: The refrigerator does a decent job of cooling, but items in the back freeze and the front glass door sweats and leaks water on the floor.  I did not think of it as a warranty item, but the manufacturer wants me to pay the freight to return it after two months and no help is available after twelve.  I am in month 14.  We will keep the item, but we do not feel it is a good value for the price.

Worked for about a year and a half. Put it in our showroom and never hardly used it. Noticed it leaking all over our hardwood floor the other day and threw it away. Leaked water and quit cooling. Got one cheaper somewhere else and bought a warranty.
control_failure: Had this unit a little over 45 days now and the thermostat went out but since day one it had a rough clicking sound in the middle of the night that made me uncomfortable to the point that I had to cock my clock.
